sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: Broadcom은 2025 회계연도에 638억 8,700만 달러의 매출과 254억 8,400만 달러의 영업이익을 기록하며 반도체 및 인프라 소프트웨어 솔루션 사업을 영위하고 있습니다.

keyPoints.text: 연구개발비는 전년 대비 1,667백만 달러(+18 %) 증가하였으며, 이는 주로 주식 보상 비용의 증가에 기인합니다.
판매관리비는 인원 감소 및 VMware 인수 관련 비용 감소로 인해 전년 대비 748백만 달러(-15 %) 감소하였습니다.
구조조정 및 기타 비용은 VMware 사업 통합과 관련된 직원 해고 비용 감소로 전년 대비 942백만 달러(-61 %) 감소하였습니다.

riskChanges.text: 정부 지원을 받는 경쟁사, 클라우드 제공업체, 오픈소스 개발자 등과의 경쟁 심화 및 제품 번들링 전략이 사업에 부정적 영향을 미칠 수 있습니다.
인수합병 및 합작 투자 전략이 운영 결과에 부정적인 영향을 줄 수 있으며, 지적재산권 보호 실패 및 제품 결함에 따른 책임 위험이 존재합니다.

businessChanges.text: 반도체 솔루션 및 인프라 소프트웨어 부문의 매출 비중은 최종 시장 수요의 변동에 따라 분기별로 변동성을 보이고 있습니다.
인수 관련 무형자산 상각비는 VMware를 제외한 이전 소프트웨어 인수 건의 고객 관련 무형자산 상각 완료로 인해 전년 대비 1,213백만 달러(-37 %) 감소하였습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 2025 회계연도 매출은 638억 8,700만 달러, 영업이익은 254억 8,400만 달러, 순이익은 231억 2,600만 달러를 기록하였습니다.
총자산은 1,710억 9,200만 달러이며, 현금 및 현금성 자산은 161억 7,800만 달러를 보유하고 있습니다.

watchItems.text: VMware 통합 비용 감소와 무형자산 상각 완료가 영업이익률 개선에 기여하고 있으나, 주식 보상 비용 증가가 R&D 비용 상승을 견인하고 있어 향후 비용 효율화 지속 여부를 모니터링해야 합니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: FY2025 revenue = 638억 8,700만 달러 (USD 63.887B; 63,887 million), period ended 2025-11-02.
FY2025 operating income = 254억 8,400만 달러 (USD 25.484B; 25,484 million), period ended 2025-11-02.
FY2025 net income = 231억 2,600만 달러 (USD 23.126B; 23,126 million), period ended 2025-11-02.
FY2025 total assets = 1,710억 9,200만 달러 (USD 171.092B; 171,092 million), period ended 2025-11-02.
FY2025 cash and cash equivalents = 161억 7,800만 달러 (USD 16.178B; 16,178 million), period ended 2025-11-02.

Competition in our industries could prevent us from growing our revenue. • Our operating results are subject to substantial quarterly and annual fluctuations. • We have pursued, and may in the future pursue, mergers, acquisitions, investments, joint ventures and dispositions, which could adversely affect our results of operations. • We may be involved in legal proceedings that could materially adversely affect our business. 12 Table of Contents • Failure to protect the IP utilized in our business could adversely affect our business. • We are subject to warranty claims, product recalls and product liability. • The complexity of our products could result in unforeseen delays or expense or undetected defects or bugs. • We are subject to privacy and data security laws and contractual commitments, and our actual or perceived failure to comply with such laws and commitments could harm our busi

We face competition from companies that receive financial and other support from their home country government, customers who develop competing products, public cloud providers, numerous smaller companies that specialize in specific aspects of the highly fragmented software industry, open source authors who provide software and IP for free, and competitors who offer their products through try-and-buy or freemium models. In addition, the trend toward consolidation is also changing the competitive landscape. We expect this trend to continue, which may result in combined competitors having greater resources than we do. The actions of our competitors, in the areas of pricing and product bundling in particular, could have a substantial adverse impact on us. Further, competitors may leverage their superior market position, as well as IP or other proprietary information, including interface, in

Revenue A majority of our net revenue is derived from sales of a broad range of semiconductor and semiconductor-based solutions that are incorporated into electronic products, as well as from modules, switches and subsystems and, in some cases, racks. Net revenue is also generated from the sale of software solutions that enable our customers to plan, develop, deliver, automate, manage, and secure applications across mainframe, distributed, edge, mobile, and private and hybrid cloud platforms. Our overall net revenue, as well as the percentage of total net revenue generated by sales in our semiconductor solutions and infrastructure software segments, have varied from quarter to quarter, due largely to fluctuations in end-market demand which are discussed in detail in Part I, Item 1A. Risk Factors of this Annual Report on Form 10-K. Distributors and original equipment manufacturers (“OEMs”

Research and Development Expense Research and development expense increased $1,667 million, or 18%, in fiscal year 2025, compared to the prior fiscal year. The increase was primarily due to higher stock-based compensation. Selling,

General and Administrative Expense Selling, general and administrative expense decreased $748 million, or 15%, in fiscal year 2025, compared to the prior fiscal year. The decrease was primarily due to lower compensation resulting from a decrease in headcount and lower VMware acquisition-related costs, partially offset by higher stock-based compensation. Amortization of Acquisition-Related Intangible Assets in

Operating Expenses Amortization of acquisition-related intangible assets recognized in operating expenses decreased $1,213 million, or 37%, in fiscal year 2025, compared to the prior fiscal year primarily due to full amortization of customer-related intangible assets from previous software acquisitions other than VMware. Restructuring and Other Charges Restructuring and other charges recognized in operating expenses decreased $942 million, or 61%, in fiscal year 2025, compared to the prior fiscal year primarily due to lower employee termination costs associated with the integration of the VMware business.
